HomeOutcomes.title: Real leadership for schools
HomeOutcomes.subtitle: A national programme—not a one-off event.
HomeOutcomes.sideImageAlt: MyCEO / NextGen CEO programme activity photo {n}
HomeOutcomes.o1d: Students practise planning, communicating, and deciding under pressure—skills that last beyond the classroom.
HomeOutcomes.o2d: Certificates and PAJSK-aligned records give families and schools clear proof of participation.
HomeOutcomes.o3d: Teams meet business mentors and judges and build confidence with guided support.
HomeOutcomes.o4d: From registration to finale—key milestones you can map to the school year.

HomeFaq.title: Questions school leaders ask
HomeFaq.subtitle: Straight answers about structure, recognition, and where to find official detail.
HomeFaq.q1: Who is NextGen CEO Challenge for?
HomeFaq.a1: The programme is designed for secondary students and schools that want a rigorous, national-level entrepreneurship and leadership experience. Your school registers as the participating institution; students compete as teams with educator support.
HomeFaq.q2: What recognition do students and schools receive?
HomeFaq.a2: Participants can earn national certificates and PAJSK-related recognition as outlined in the official awards and terms. Exact categories and conditions are published on the Competition hub so you can share them with leadership and parents.
HomeFaq.q3: How much time does the programme take?
HomeFaq.a3: The season runs from registration through bootcamp, pitching milestones, and the grand finale window. The timeline page lists key dates so you can map commitments for teachers and student teams.
HomeFaq.q4: Where are the official rules and judging criteria?
HomeFaq.a4: All governing documents — terms, judging breakdown, and participation steps — live on the Competition section of this site. We keep a single hub so you are never hunting across PDFs or unofficial copies.
HomeFaq.a5: Use “Register your school” to reach the official registration flow. If you need clarification before committing, use the Contact page and we will route you through the organiser’s official channels.

HomeFinalCta.title: Secure your school’s place on the national stage
HomeFinalCta.body: Join a cohort of schools investing in leadership, entrepreneurship, and recognised achievement for their students.

HomeApaItu.title: What is Next Gen CEO?
HomeApaItu.afterPak: and open hands-on leadership & entrepreneurship learning.

ManfaatPelajar.title: Benefits for students
ManfaatPelajar.grandDesc: Certificate of recognition & access to professional mentors
ManfaatPelajar.c2Desc: Students are tested on strategy, problem-solving, and real pitching like young CEOs.
ManfaatPelajar.c3Desc: Compete with selected students nationwide and strengthen your profile.
ManfaatPelajar.c4Desc: Sharpened to think critically, decide quickly, and collaborate as a team.
ManfaatPelajar.c5Desc: Meet professional mentors, industry judges, and talented peers — and open future doors.

ManfaatSekolah.title: Benefits for schools
ManfaatSekolah.m1d: Official participation that boosts credibility and national standing.
ManfaatSekolah.m2d: Build a positive perception as an active, progressive, competitive school.
ManfaatSekolah.m3d: A enrichment platform that supports co-curricular development strategically.
ManfaatSekolah.m4d: Students and schools access professional mentors, entrepreneurs, and partners.
ManfaatSekolah.m5d: Participants can return as peer mentors and drivers of leadership culture.

Terma.title: Competition terms & conditions
Terma.i1p: Open to selected invited secondary schools.
Terma.i2p: All registrations must go through the organiser’s official channels.
Terma.i3p: The team of four must be maintained throughout the programme.
Terma.i4p: Each team must complete every judging component during the competition.
Terma.i5p: Each team must have four lower secondary students with one teacher chaperone. Schools may enter more than one team.
Terma.i6p: Information must be complete & accurate. The organiser may reject invalid entries.
Terma.i8p: Judged by a panel; decisions are final.

Penilaian.title: Judging structure — NextGen CEO Challenge 2026
Penilaian.s1p: Team energy, collaboration, communication, and task sharing.
Penilaian.s2p: Originality of product/idea, value add, and innovation potential.
Penilaian.s3p: Sales effectiveness, promotion, and real customer interaction.
Penilaian.s4p: Delivery, confidence, pitch structure, and Q&A with the judges.

Timeline.s1desc: Register via official channels & confirm your team.
Timeline.s2desc: Team collaboration and innovative ideas are assessed.
Timeline.s3desc: Mini sales & final pitch: real-market performance and how you present your idea.

Educator.p1: As educators, we know true excellence is no longer measured by exam papers alone.
Educator.p2Lead: NextGen CEO is designed to complement your school ecosystem with
Educator.p2Tail: and real-world application. We help teachers spot and sharpen hidden leadership talent—turning passive potential into active competence.
Educator.quote: Our job isn’t only to teach—it’s to build a platform where every student can think like a leader and act like an innovator.
Educator.footnote: *Limited intake to maintain mentor guidance quality.

ApaItu.title: What is Next Gen CEO?
ApaItu.lead: Next Gen CEO is an initiative by MyCEO Education to support the Malaysia Education Development Plan and 21st Century Skills (PAK21) while giving high-potential students practical leadership and entrepreneurship experience.
ApaItu.c1d: Helps students analyse, evaluate, and solve problems wisely.
ApaItu.c2d: Builds confidence and leadership in real situations.

AboutPage.title: About us
AboutPage.subtitle: MyCEO — shaping a new generation bold enough to lead tomorrow's economy.
AboutPage.kicker: Here, young minds are forged with entrepreneurial wisdom—creative, confident, commanding—so they are not merely employees, but employers and catalysts of change for the era ahead.
AboutPage.body1: Welcome to MyCEO—a space where the next generation is shaped into business leaders with a sharp entrepreneurial mindset: sharp thinking, brave creation, confident action, and authoritative leadership. Beyond that, they are mentored systematically to carry the baton as employers and drivers of innovation in the economic landscape of the future.
AboutPage.body2: Through MyCEO's entrepreneurship education programme, each child's interests, talents, and multiple intelligences are surfaced, refined, and developed so their potential is maximised with purpose. From an early age, they are introduced step by step to the world of business—not only to know it, but to care deeply—so that entrepreneurship becomes a serious, meaningful career choice when they enter adulthood. Alongside this, they are trained to become confident, strategic, and skilled leaders ready for real-world challenges.
AboutPage.body3: Our ambition is clear: to nurture a generation of genius—leaders who succeed not only in the economy, but who propel society and the nation toward sustainable progress, so Malaysia stands shoulder to shoulder in global competition and sets the pace in innovation and entrepreneurship.
AboutPage.historyIntro: Three chapters in our journey building generations of young entrepreneurs.
AboutPage.historyMilestone1Body: Since 2014, MyCEO (Malaysia Children Entrepreneurship Orientation Program) has been a pioneer in nurturing a generation of young people with an entrepreneurial spirit. Beginning as a CSR project, MyCEO has successfully run numerous programmes with strong support from leading organisations including the Ministry of Education Malaysia, Ministry of Youth & Sports, DPMM, UPSI, UPM, SME Corp, Bursa Malaysia, RHB Bank, and many more.
AboutPage.historyMilestone2Body: The outstanding response from parents and teachers every year encouraged us to go further. The result was MyCEO Academy, which now offers innovative programmes tailored to children and teenagers in the world of entrepreneurship.
AboutPage.historyMilestone3Body: In March 2020, this journey continued with the establishment of MyCEO Education Sdn Bhd—a decisive step to broaden our impact and reach even more young people.
AboutPage.programsSubtitle: Structured experiences that introduce children and teens to business, money, and real-world selling—in a fun, age-appropriate way.
AboutPage.programBucDesc: A hands-on school-holiday workshop where children learn business basics, build confidence, and practise real buying and selling in a lively, supportive setting.
AboutPage.programBucImageAlt: Bengkel Usahawan Cilik — school holiday sales and pitching programme
AboutPage.programMskDesc: A structured programme that teaches children to manage money, understand needs versus wants, and build strong financial habits from an early age.
AboutPage.programMskImageAlt: Money Smart Kids — financial literacy for children
AboutPage.programKkucDesc: An interactive camp and carnival where kids become “little bosses”—handling capital, selling products, and trading in a real-world mini bazaar atmosphere.
AboutPage.programKkucImageAlt: Kem & Karnival Usahawan Cilik — children’s entrepreneurship showcase
AboutPage.gallerySubtitle: Highlights from our programmes, events, and community—moments that capture the spirit of young leadership and entrepreneurship.
AboutPage.partnersSubtitle: Together nurturing the entrepreneurs of tomorrow
AboutPage.partnersLogosAlt: Official partner logos: Yayasan Uniti, Bursa Malaysia, Encorp, PPAS, and YAWAS.
AboutPage.partnersBannerAlt: Partner logos: Yayasan Uniti, Bursa Malaysia, Encorp, PPAS (Perbadanan Perpustakaan Awam Selangor), and YAWAS (Yayasan Warisan Anak Selangor).
